app_info.title: RPi Spectrophotometer
app_info.description: AS726X Spectrophotometry Software + MLX90640 Thermal Camera - Web Version
app_info.desc: Django Web Version for Raspberry Pi with dual I2C bus

charts.no_analysis_results: No results. Load files and process data.
charts.approx_warn: Approximation valid for n≤50. For higher precision use statistical software.

history.title: Measurements History
history.content:
Measurements History

Stores a high-density log of all captures (Single, Continuous, or Sequential). It includes raw and calculated data (Absorbance and Transmittance). During continuous measurement, for performance reasons, the table will only display the last 100 events, but all recorded events will be exported to CSV completely.

comms_log.title: Communications Log
comms_log.content:
System and Hardware Log

Audit window at the I2C bus level. Displays internal equipment events: connection to the Raspberry Pi, calibration confirmations, warnings regarding potential thermal or spectral hardware errors, and software events detected on the fly.

numeric_display.title: Last Recorded Values
numeric_display.content:
Last Recorded Values

Immediate cross-spectral feedback matrix.

Displays the raw vector of counted photons. If activated, subtraction obeys I_net = I_sample - I_dark. Technical observation of these values allows predicting when the underlying photonic CCD of the AS726X reaches its optical saturation level (full 16 bits). If you notice clipping near raw limits, reduce the LED emitter intensity or apply initial dilutions.

beer_lambert.title: Concentration Calculation (Beer-Lambert Law)
beer_lambert.content:
Concentration Calculation (Beer-Lambert Law)

Pure derivative of the macroscopic electromagnetic absorption theorem:


A = ε · b · C    ⇒    C = A / (ε · b)


  • A (Dimensionless Absorbance): Logarithm of the photonic decay fraction, calculated as A = -log₁₀(I / I₀).
  • ε (Molar Absorptivity Coefficient): Analyte sensitivity in L · mol⁻¹ · cm⁻¹.
  • b (Optical Path): Depth of the ray incidenting the analytical structure, typically 1.0 cm in cuvettes.

Empirical use prioritizes the OLS statistical crossover where the machine explicitly replaces (ε·b) with the Slope (m) pushed by calibration.

ref_cal_config_group.title: Configure Reference Calibration
ref_cal_config_group.content:
Reference Calibration Mathematics

Secondary algorithmic processing of "indirect calibration" forcing the Y-intercept correction to zero (Direct) or absorbing latent variables.

A_ref = m·C + b   or   A_ref = (Factor)·C

The "Direct Factor" imposes a zero intercept restriction (b = 0). Physically assumes that at zero concentration there is 0 absorbance, eliminating spurious stray light from the equation but carrying errors if the hardware has factory optical blindness (high dark current).

blank_subtraction.title: Blank Subtraction
blank_subtraction.content:
Differential Blank Subtraction Theorem

Mathematical implementation of the collateral environmental noise rejection filter. Allows computing true Transmittance in a vector space.

T(λ) = ( I_sample(λ) - I_dark(λ) ) / ( I_ref(λ) - I_dark(λ) )

Ignoring the blank factor propagates severe non-linearities at low concentrations (Tyndall scattering effects of the solvent itself).

plot_type.title: Plot Type
plot_type.content:
How do you want to see the chart? (Plot Types)

We don't always see things the same way. Here you change the lens through which you look at light:

  • RAW Count (Pure Photons): Literally how many "hits" of light the electric sensor receives. Measures Intensity. If you cover the sensor it will drop to almost 0. If you put it in the sun, it will rise to 65,000 (burns or saturates). Use it only to calibrate your flashlight.
  • Transmittance (%T): What percentage of light managed to cross to the other side of the container? 100% is crystal clear water. 0% is a black brick.
  • Absorbance (A): The laboratory king! Mathematically measures "how much light the liquid ate". Calculated with a logarithm (-log T). The magic of Absorbance is that if you put twice as much chemical, the Absorbance will rise exactly twice (Perfect Linear Rule).

plot_customization.title: Plot Customization
plot_customization.content:
Tensorial Chart Management

Allows Multiple Coupling to the DOM cache for visual qualitative review.

Y-axis Logarithmic Scaling: The topographic equation is graphically transformed into Log10 base, compressing massive decay peaks and unveiling minor resonances undetectable visually in a flat Eulerian plot. Its use is critical when auditing the thermal matrix and noise at low Raw Intensity.

chart_tools.title: Chart Tools
chart_tools.content:
Graphic Geometric Processing (Tactile Tools)

  • Variance Forks (±σ): Dynamically computes:
    σ = √[ Σ(x_i - μ)² / (n - 1) ] to plot indicative Gaussian uncertainty vector bands in static multiple readings.
  • Automatic Annotator (λmax): Local subroutine argmax function. Identifies and continuously plots in which λ the upper differential point currently sits. Fundamental for identifying kinetic Bathochromic (Red-shifts) of changing analytes.

spectrum_live_stats.title: Spectral Statistics
spectrum_live_stats.content:
Live Statistics (The Tremor Detector)

When you watch the chart in Continuous mode, it seems to "tremble", right? It's normal, electrons jump randomly due to ambient heat and statics. Here we measure if that tremor is acceptable by studying all records instantly:

  • Coefficient of Variation (%CV):
    %CV = ( σ / μ ) × 100
    Tells you how stable the machine is. If your %CV is < 5%, you can jump for joy, your data is golden! If it exceeds 15%, stop! You have problems: someone moved the table, bubbles are floating in the container or the cable is sparking loose.
  • SNR (Signal to Noise):
    SNR = μ_signal / σ_noise
    Sometimes the sample absorbs so much that almost no light arrives. If the little light that arrives (Signal) is almost equal to the "black" static noise of the world, this metric will drop significantly, warning you not to trust the current result.

thermal_stats.title: Thermal Statistics
thermal_stats.content:
Superficial Macro-Thermal Processing

Synthesizes incoming blackbody radiation information to the MLX90640 focal sensor. Algorithmically projects the global constant deriving the maximum thermal asilar point [T_max] against its structural minimum [T_min], promediando el cuadro ambiente térmico absoluto (μ_temp). Ideal for confirming general isotherm of containers.

conc_controls.title: Curve Controls
conc_controls.content:
Sampling and OLS M-Nx Matrix Subroutines

The bridge between in-situ control and regressive matrix: By entering the experimental base axiom (X) on screen, the strict value of the actual Absorbance response detected in the container (Y) is "Captured" at the photon level. An indexed pair (x_i, y_i) is built with each Click to fill the subsequent ordinal least squares matrix.

chart_tools_conc.title: Geometric Explanation: Bands and Whiskers
chart_tools_conc.content:
Basic Geometric Explanation: Bands and Whiskers

Taking 1 measurement is easy. Taking 5 on the same drop might give you minutely different values due to pure noise. For this, advanced tools are used:

  • Bars ±σ (Pure Variance or Deviation): Draws "whiskers" on each measurement point.
    σ = √[ Σ(x_i - μ)² / (n - 1) ].
    If the 'whisker' is very high (high voltage/instability) do not insert that point, repeat it. Ideal if you did replicates in Excel.
  • 95% Confidence Interval (Purple CI): Draws a thin sleeve flush with the general mean line. Demonstrates the "mathematical safe path" where theory suggests the calibrated matrix should navigate with 95% inferential security.
  • 95% Prediction Interval (Orange IP): This widened super-sleeve tries to hunt your next natural error. It tells a stranger: "Look, if you measure a new sample here, there is 95% certainty the point will fall within this giant orange band".

regression_stats.title: Regression Statistics
regression_stats.content:
What do the Regression Letters and Numbers mean?

Here the machine does the work of a statistics professor evaluating the curve to give you peace of mind. Read the guide below if you are confused:

  • R² and Adjusted R²: The Determination Coefficient. A number ranging from 0 to 1. It means: How tightly/closely did your actual points adhere to the drawn ideal magic line? A 1.0 is absolutely perfect and superimposed. If it's 0.5 or less your data is a cloud of disaster and not useful for measuring anything.
  • RMSE and SEE: Quadratic Errors. They simply count the "average of the gross deviation" that the points suffered moving up and down from the reference line center and deliver a "Penalty" in the same unit you put for Y.
  • LOD and LOQ (Detection Limits): If the measurement liquid is just clear distilled water will it give mathematical zero? No! The electronic diode vibrates sending residual noise to the PC. LOD calculates the minimum blind white noise the equipment will throw (~ 3σ) preventing you from measuring ghosts or invisible traces. LOQ is the safe threshold from where you can start measuring and publishing papers without being embarrassed (~ 10σ) and they are calculated by dividing the dark fluctuation over your actual calibrated strength (LOD = 3.3xσ_b / slope).

conc_points.title: Curve Points
conc_points.content:
Multivariable Curve Point Vector Cache

Static reflection of all temporary two-dimensional matrix memory (X_i, Y_i). Used for auditing, manually depurating destructive outliers or resetting the internal regression framework due to general numerical poisoning.

cal_view.title: Select View
cal_view.content:
Topological Transitions (Abs vs Ref Maps)

Transverse matrix pivot. Alternates between the natural absolute correlation (A vs Concentration) and the crossed or empirical coupling map (A_machine vs A_reference) which allows carrying universal chemical libraries extracting noise correction factors in secondary equipment.

cal_add_points.title: Add Points
cal_add_points.content:
Standard / Spike Sample Injection

Real-time empirical calibration accelerator. Supports manual sequential queuing of progressive volumetric dilutions to the AS726X cubicle to retro-align an exact coordinate X-Y axis. Each time you inject diluent and press Add, you register the final photometry.

chart_tools_cal.title: Variance and Geometry in Transfer
chart_tools_cal.content:
Render Variance and Geometry in Transfer

Visually projects, renders, and interpolates the raw results from the OLS-Student backend, generating the CI and PI envelopes in the Transfer Calibration phase to audit instrumental setup failures between the standard device and your local photometer.

cal_results.title: Results and Actions
cal_results.content:
Retrospective OLS Certification

Linkage motor with the active session. Generates the App Session Overwrite algorithmic bridge injecting the extracted mathematics [ slope, intercept, residual spectroscopic correction ] back into the on-the-fly calculations operated in the "Concentration and Measurement Controls".

analytical_validation.title: Analytical Validation
analytical_validation.content:
Analytical Validation (The Machine's Final Exam)

How do you know if your calibration was good? By making the machine try to guess a container you already know! This is called Weighted Efficiency Factor (Recovery Rate).

How does it work? You take the line you just created and feed the light of your own standard sample back into it, solving the formula to see how much Concentration the machine predicts. Then, you compare the prediction against reality.

  • 100%: Perfection. The machine reads exactly what you prepared.
  • Below 95%: Something went wrong. Maybe the liquid reacted, lost color, or the tube was dirty.
  • Above 105%: Careful. There is "white noise", a false extra color (stray light, or something cloudy floating) adding lies to the absorbance.

quality_control.title: Quality Control
quality_control.content:
Quality Control Assistant (Westgard Rules)

In serious hospitals and laboratories, a human doesn't decide if a test is good; an uncompromising machine does using Rules. Here you have your own limiting assistant (Go/No-Go):

  • Red Flag (Failed): If your R² is less than 0.99, or the points have mathematically unacceptable variance, the conditional rule stops everything. Do not measure real samples with this curve! Destroy the data and start again by cleaning the container well.
  • Green Flag (Validated): Implies you passed all probabilistic filters. You can use your curve blindly in the real world.

cal_points.title: Calibration Points
cal_points.content:
Persistent I/O Calibration Point Base

Visualizer in locally editable cell matrix. Allows safeguarding current OLS matrix object state in disc memory with CSV files, protecting arduous parametric hours of work from instrumental power outages or carrying over validated previous month's calibrations.

thermal_control.title: Thermal Control
thermal_control.content:
Infrared Camera Controls

Here you decide how the "predatory night mode" or thermal vision of your lab turns on. You ensure turning the camera bus on and off safely.

What is the Start Measurement button for? By pressing it, you tell the camera: "Ignore the simple live view, start recording methodically every centigrade change that occurs and package it for me to send and print in Excel later."

Automatic Auto Scale: The 2D chart is forced to redraw its colors stretching like an elastic band always to cover the hottest and coldest point of the container. Watch out! Disable it if you plan to compare "before vs after" serialized, because otherwise the tones will deceive you.

thermal_advanced.title: Advanced Statistics
thermal_advanced.content:
Thermal Statistics (Cleaning Lying Pixels)

Sometimes, 1 of the 768 pixels on the camera reads something silly (like the reflection of a hot light or is factory defective). To prevent it from damaging your average reading, we use defensive mathematics:

  • Rigid Median (μ_mid): Instead of a silly average that adds the pixel with error, group the 768 values from lowest to highest and pick exactly the middle one. A pixel at 1000°C will not affect this value.
  • 10th and 90th Percentile (Cutting the edges): Ignores the 10% lowest and 10% highest thermal pixels. We stay only with the real thermal core (the central 80% of the container) discarding millimetric external noise at the edge.

thermal_measurement.title: Data Measurement
thermal_measurement.content:
Kinetic Acquirer (Recording Temperature Change)

If you want to demonstrate how a chemical cools or heats up on its own for 5 minutes, put this engine in motion. It will create a history recording how the Mean or Percentiles behave over time and allow you to download a nice "Time-Lapse" or CSV in Excel. Mathematically extracts the focal matrix and calculates the variance over (Δ time).

analysis_files.title: Load CSV Files
analysis_files.content:
Post-Mortem Multivariable Input Aggregator

Virtual structure that loads raw binary/CSV files from "N" runs or photometry repetitions in parallel to the cache. It is the philosopher's stone to create the comparative scaffold of "N files vs Lambda or Time", requiring a manual "Identifier" to stack coherent data.

analysis_params.title: Parameters
analysis_params.content:
Reference Null Variable Motor (μ₀ Test)

Selects through which physical λ slot all inter-month transversal parametric diagnostics of the apilated N files will be applied. Formally introduces the theoretical Null Hypothesis (μ₀) to rigorously test it against your entire real saved experimental repetition pack (Example μ₀ = 1.00 Theoretical exact Absorbance imposed to validate empirically).

analysis_stats.title: Advanced Statistics Suite
analysis_stats.content:
Advanced Parametric Variance and Inferential Calculation (SciPy Engine)

Master matrix operator for formal statistical control (superior QC):

  • Shapiro-Wilk D-Evaluator (W statistic):
    Tests normal population distribution inference. Calculates the correlation of the set of standardized residuals regarding the expected Blind-Normal Quantile. If the W value drops (P-Value < 0.05), it repudiates the base normality, making the ±σ and Confidence tests a non-reliable mirage.
  • Student's Univariate t-Test (t value):
    t = ( x̄ - μ₀ ) / ( s / √n )
    Scientifically tests to what extent the average deviation is mathematically unforgivable or simply a harmful effect of a very small noisy sample. Provides a definitive verdict (P-value inferred) ready to be included in an academic report.
  • Skew/Kurt Drift Metrics: Calculate directional lateral bias (short or long tails) exposing constant human errors if they permanently bias the instrumental residuals in broken or scratched containers to a single axis.

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
introduction.content:
1. Introduction

This application allows controlling a spectrophotometer based on the AS726X sensor and an MLX90640 thermal camera on a Raspberry Pi with double I2C bus.

Sensor Connections:

  • MLX90640 (Thermal Camera) – I2C-1: SDA → GPIO2, SCL → GPIO3
  • AS726X (Spectrophotometer) – I2C-22: SDA → GPIO23, SCL → GPIO24
  • External LED: VIN → GPIO17

advanced_statistics_suite.title: Advanced Statistics Suite
advanced_statistics_suite.content:
Advanced Statistics Suite

The application integrates analytical-level statistical tools for spectral and thermal signal processing:

  • Real-Time Descriptive Statistics: Instant calculation of means, standard deviation (σ), %CV (Coefficient of Variation), skewness, and kurtosis. This allows evaluating spectral dispersion on the fly.
  • Analytical Performance Metrics: Automatic calculation of the Limit of Detection (LOD = 3σ/m) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ = 10σ/m), crucial for validating calibration sensitivity.
  • Advanced Linear Regression: Extracts R², adjusted R², RMSE (Root Mean Square Error), and Standard Error of the Estimate (SEE). Evaluates real fit weighting degrees of freedom.
  • Confidence and Prediction Intervals (95% CI/PI): Graphical tool that delimits uncertainty (stochastic error bands) around calibration curves based on the t-Student statistic.
  • Quality Control (QC): Semaphore system that evaluates configurable rules (e.g., R² > 0.99, %CV < 5%) to ensure reliable results before sample quantification.
  • Hypothesis Testing: Distributional analysis (Shapiro-Wilk) to determine data normality, and parametric t-test to evaluate significance against reference values (μ₀).

connection.title: Connection (I2C RPI)
connection.content: Select your I2C mode. For hardware running on Raspberry Pi, select I2C Direct. To simulate measurements on a PC, select Emulator.

measurement.title: Measurement Control
measurement.content: Configure how measurements are taken. You can average multiple spectra (M) for a single point, or take continuous/sequential readings.

data_management.title: Data Management
data_management.content: Provides options to export the current session data as a CSV file, or clear the entire history.

export_graph.subtitle: Configure the details of your graph to ensure a professional and informative report.
export_graph.metadata: Include metadata and detailed results table (Date, Mode, B-S, Thermal Camera)
